entries.statement: Knowledge of enterprise data strategy frameworks, including organizational data vision, strategic data objectives, and alignment between data governance and business strategy.
Knowledge of enterprise board-level risk reporting and integration of data risk, AI risk, and technology risk into executive risk dashboards and board governance.

Knowledge of data governance program design and operating models, including federated, centralized, and hub-and-spoke governance structures, governance council architectures, and organizational design patterns.
Knowledge of data catalog and metadata management platforms, including active metadata principles, automated lineage tracking, and business glossary governance.
Knowledge of data maturity assessment frameworks, including DCAM, DAMA DMBOK, and CMMI-DMM, and how maturity scores translate to organizational investment priorities.
Knowledge of AI model lifecycle governance, including model versioning strategies, performance monitoring, drift detection, retraining decision logic, and decommissioning procedures.
Knowledge of data policy authoring and governance, including policy structure standards, approval workflows, enforcement mechanisms, exception handling, and policy lifecycle management.
Knowledge of data domain ownership and stewardship models, including domain boundary definition, accountability assignment, and cross-domain governance dispute resolution.
Knowledge of specific data domain context, including business purpose, system sources, consumer use cases, data lineage, and applicable regulatory classifications.
Knowledge of DataOps frameworks and operational data management, including data pipeline reliability, data quality monitoring, data freshness SLAs, and continuous operational improvement.
Knowledge of data architecture governance, including architecture-level governance decisions, data service design patterns, and alignment of data architecture with organizational objectives.
Knowledge of data minimization and privacy-focused governance, including privacy impact assessments, data minimization policies, personal data protection, and privacy-by-design implementation.
Knowledge of enterprise information management frameworks, including information architecture principles, enterprise-level knowledge management systems, and information accessibility standards.

Knowledge of AI testing, monitoring, and incident management governance, including testing infrastructure, post-market monitoring design, incident response procedures, and performance evaluation governance.
Knowledge of AI supply chain risk governance, including third-party AI component assessment, supplier risk evaluation, intellectual property risk, and supply chain governance policies.
Knowledge of data classification frameworks and standards, including data sensitivity levels, classification methodologies, and regulatory classification requirements.
Knowledge of regulatory compliance and legal frameworks for data and AI systems, including regulatory requirement identification, compliance obligation mapping, and compliance strategy development.
Knowledge of AI risk governance structures and processes, including risk governance roles and responsibilities, risk communication frameworks, and organizational risk tolerance definition.
Knowledge of data asset management principles, including data asset inventory methods, data source documentation, data provenance tracking, and asset lifecycle management.
Knowledge of data administration policies and procedures, including data access governance, data usage monitoring, and operational data management governance.
Knowledge of data standardization and data quality governance, including data standards development, data quality metrics, and standardization enforcement mechanisms.
Knowledge of data security governance and access control policies, including data security markings, access control frameworks, and data handling procedures for sensitive data.
Knowledge of data warehousing and data integration governance, including data warehouse architecture governance, integration patterns, and cross-system data governance.
Knowledge of AI data governance, including training data governance, data provenance for AI systems, data preparation governance, and data governance for model development.
Knowledge of enterprise risk management frameworks for risk identification, risk assessment methodologies, and risk mitigation strategy development.
Knowledge of organizational data governance roles and accountability structures, including steward roles, governance council design, and accountability frameworks.
Knowledge of data governance performance measurement and continuous improvement, including governance metrics, capability maturity assessment, and governance improvement planning.
